page: Google Cloud Platform n1-highmem-64 is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance powered by an Intel Xeon x86_64 processor. It features 64 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 32 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, operating at a base clock speed of 2.3 GHz. The system is equipped with 416 GB of system memory, providing 6.5 GB of RAM per vCPU. It does not include local SSD storage or bundled GPUs, and has zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data indicates a clear distinction between single-core and multi-core performance. Single-core metrics, including stress-ng and Geekbench single-core tests, fall into the weak category, reflecting the older 2.3 GHz Intel Xeon architecture. However, multi-core performance scales effectively, achieving average ratings in stress-ng multi-core, Passmark CPU, and Geekbench multi-core benchmarks. Memory bandwidth tests for both cached and uncached reads and writes also perform in the average tier, with uncached read speeds reaching 80.73 GB/sec.

Given its high memory capacity and average multi-threaded performance, the n1-highmem-64 is suited for memory-bound workloads such as database operations, Redis caching layers, and software compilation. It achieved 39.4 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the instance provides a high density of memory relative to vCPUs, making it a viable option for legacy enterprise applications and memory-heavy workloads that do not require high single-threaded performance or local NVMe storage.
description: Google Cloud Platform n1-highmem-64 is an x86_64 virtual machine configured with 64 dedicated vCPUs and 416 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon processor running at 2.3 GHz. This instance does not include local storage or GPUs. Performance benchmarks show a contrast between weak single-core execution and average multi-core capabilities, with the instance scoring in the middle 50% for multi-threaded tasks, memory bandwidth, and database operations. It is qualitatively cost-effective for memory-intensive workloads that benefit from a high memory-to-vCPU ratio but do not demand the highest single-threaded CPU speeds. The n1-highmem-64 is best suited for databases, caching systems like Redis, and multi-threaded enterprise applications.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n1-highmem-64 is an x86_64 virtual server featuring 64 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 416 GB of RAM. It is designed for memory-intensive workloads requiring moderate multi-core performance.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n1-highmem-64 is an x86_64 server with 64 vCPUs and 416 GB RAM, optimized for memory-intensive database and caching workloads.
tagline: A memory-dense virtual server featuring sixty-four dedicated cores and average multi-threaded performance for database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated vCPUs and 416 GB of system memory
Intel Xeon x86_64 processor running at 2.3 GHz
Average multi-core and memory bandwidth performance
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Optimized for databases, Redis caching, and memory-heavy workloads
